name: Road Construction
tagline: Engineered surfaces and drainage that make wheeled transport reliable in wet seasons and heavy freight
problem:
Soil turns to mud under wheel loads; ruts trap vehicles; slopes erode under rain. Paths sufficient for feet fail under tonnes. The road problem is distributing wheel pressure into subgrade without pumping fines upward, shedding water sideways before it softens the foundation, and maintaining crossfall for drainage.

Roads are also social technology: width standards, right-of-way law, tolls, and maintenance labour allocation determine whether a route stays passable across political seasons.

Stone Roman roads and modern asphalt differ in materials but share layering logic: subgrade, base, wearing course — each layer sized for grain distribution and compaction.
overview:
A durable unpaved road begins with clearing organics, cutting a bench into hillside for positive drainage, and installing a crown — high centre, low edges — so water runs off. Aggregate layers use graded stone: large stones at bottom, smaller toward top, fines to lock mechanically when compacted. Corduroy logs bridge bogs historically.

Paved roads add a bound surface: Roman concrete with pozzolan, later macadam with angular crushed stone locked by compaction, later tar macadam, later hot-mix asphalt on prepared base. Each step improves impermeability and ride but increases maintenance tooling needs.

Drainage structures — culverts, ditches, retaining walls — often matter more than surface quality. Plugged culverts destroy paved roads from underneath.

Surveying aligns grades: too steep exhausts draft animals and trucks; too flat ponds water. Transition curves reduce jerk for fast traffic later.

principles.name: Subgrade bearing capacity
principles.explanation: Weak subgrades fail by shear; road thickness spreads load over wider footprint — structural number concepts formalise this in modern pavement engineering.

principles.name: Frost heave and capillary rise
principles.explanation: In cold climates, water wicks into base, freezes, lifts surface — requiring drainage layers and cut depth below frost line when possible.

principles.name: Compaction and optimum moisture
principles.explanation: Soils compact best at specific moisture contents; too dry lacks lubrication, too wet pumps under roller loads.

principles.name: Erosion on slopes
principles.explanation: Sheet flow velocity scours shoulders — energy dissipators and vegetation stabilise cuts.

components.name: Subgrade
components.function: Founding soil or rock prepared to design modulus
components.criticalNote: Proof-roll with loaded cart to find soft spots before surfacing

components.name: Base course
components.function: Distributes wheel loads; drains water laterally in permeable designs
components.criticalNote: Segregation during placement ruins grading — mix in lifts

components.name: Wearing course
components.madeFrom: Asphalt concrete, stone pavers, or compacted gravel
components.criticalNote: Crossfall 1–2% typical for drainage — too steep discomforts slow traffic

components.name: Ditches and culverts
components.function: Removes water from structure before saturation
components.position: Parallel to alignment and at low points
components.criticalNote: Maintenance access — if culverts cannot be cleaned, they will fail

rawMaterials.name: Crushed stone
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Crush-screen to gradation; wash if clay-coated

rawMaterials.name: Binder (asphalt era)
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Heat mix plants; compaction before cool

rawMaterials.name: Geotextile (modern optional)
rawMaterials.purpose: Separates fines from base, reducing pumping
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Woven polymer from in-situ chemical plants

buildSteps.title: Survey centreline and grades
buildSteps.description: Stake alignment; compute cuts and fills; locate drainage outlets that do not flood downstream users.
buildSteps.warningNote: Working near roads — visibility vests even in premodern reenactment contexts with modern traffic nearby

buildSteps.title: Strip topsoil and compact subgrade
buildSteps.description: Remove organics that decay into voids. Moisture-condition and compact in lifts with rollers or ramming.
buildSteps.warningNote: Trench collapse in deep cuts — bench slopes

buildSteps.title: Place base in lifts
buildSteps.description: Spread aggregate in thin layers; compact each until refusal; check crown with straightedge.
buildSteps.warningNote: Overthick lifts do not compact internally — false economy

buildSteps.title: Install drainage structures before surfacing
buildSteps.description: Bed culverts on firm foundation; backfill symmetrically; test flow before paving over.
buildSteps.warningNote: Plugged culverts are the fastest road destroyer

buildSteps.title: Surface and edge protect
buildSteps.description: Lay wearing course; compact; stabilise shoulders with grass or stone to prevent undermining.
prerequisiteTools: pavers or asphalt crew if modern
buildSteps.warningNote: Hot asphalt burns — PPE and escape routes

history.event: Engineered multi-layer roads with drainage and milestones enable administrative and military mobility across Europe and the Mediterranean.
Macadamised roads spread widely as industrial stone-crushing makes graded broken stone affordable; later centuries add petroleum binders to create modern flexible asphalt pavements.

inventors.name: John Loudon McAdam
inventors.contribution: Promoted crushed-stone road surfacing with drainage discipline — 'macadamisation'

impact:
Roads are the physical API between vehicles and territory. Their quality determines energy cost of logistics — shaping cities, empires, and famine relief reach.

Maintenance is the hidden half of road technology; building without a maintenance cadence is performative infrastructure.
